overview: Amaroo School's Apollo Program aims to meet the social-emotional and intellectual learning needs of students identified as gifted or gifted and talented. The program develops identified students through strategic class placements with like-minded peers and differentiation of curriculum and instruction to optimise their learning and development.
description: The Apollo Program is available to all students from Kindergarten to Year 10 who are identified as gifted or gifted and talented. Amaroo School identifies students through a range of objective and subjective measures including observations, assessments, and teacher recommendations. Students identified for the program are grouped according to giftedness as well as giftedness and talent, recognizing that some students are gifted but do not necessarily demonstrate their talents. The program operates within the mainstream school curriculum with differentiated provision rather than withdrawal programs. Students receive strategic class placements and differentiated curriculum and instruction tailored to their learning needs. The identification process is ongoing throughout the school year, and parents can contact the Apollo Program coordinator at info@amaroos.act.edu.au for more information about placement and program details.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be identified as gifted or gifted and talented through objective and subjective assessment measures; Assessment may include observations, teacher recommendations, and standardized testing; Open to all students from Kindergarten to Year 10; Gifted students demonstrate potential to perform significantly beyond majority of peers in one or more skill areas such as language, problem solving, physical or interpersonal skills
year_levels: Kindergarten, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Gifted and talented identification is based on a range of objective and subjective measures. Program provides differentiated learning through curriculum adjustment and strategic class placement rather than removal from mainstream classes. Trained teaching staff develop strategies specific to identified gifted and talented students. Identification can occur throughout the school year. For more information, contact the Apollo Program coordinator at info@amaroos.act.edu.au.

overview: Burgmann offers the Music Scholarship to students in Middle School or Senior School who demonstrate meritorious achievement and potential in music. The scholarship is funded by a generous community donor and provides individual music tuition support for 12 months.
description: Burgmann's Music Scholarship is an annual award offered thanks to the generosity of a community donor. The scholarship is available to students in either Middle School or Senior School and is awarded based on meritorious achievement and potential in instrumental music performance. This scholarship provides individual music tuition for a duration of 12 months and offers a remission of 80% of music tuition fees and associated costs, which includes exam fees, books/materials, accompanist fees, and instrument hire charges as required for one academic year. The scholarship recognises students with demonstrated musical talent and commitment to their instrumental learning.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Middle School or Senior School; Demonstrated meritorious achievement and musical potential; Enrolled in instrumental music program at Burgmann
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded on the basis of meritorious achievement and potential in music. The remission covers 80% of music tuition fees and associated costs including exam fees, books/materials, accompanist fees, and instrument hire charges for one academic year. Scholarship is for the duration of 12 months and may be subject to annual review and renewal based on ongoing musical performance and engagement.

overview: Burgmann Anglican School offers the Music Scholarship, an award provided through the generosity of a community donor. The scholarship is available to students in Middle School or Senior School and recognises meritorious achievement and potential in music.
description: The Music Scholarship provides individual music tuition for a duration of 12 months, offering remission of 80% of music tuition fees and associated costs. This includes exam fees, books and materials, accompanist fees, and instrument hire charges as required for one academic year. The scholarship is awarded based on meritorious achievement and potential in music performance. Selection is determined by the school's music department assessment of student musical ability and commitment to instrumental learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Middle School or Senior School; Demonstrated meritorious achievement and potential in music; Must be enrolled as a student at Burgmann Anglican School
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship provides 12 months of remission for music tuition fees and associated costs including exam fees, books/materials, accompanist fees, and instrument hire charges as required for one academic year.

school_name: Canberra Grammar School - Northside Infants Campus

overview: Canberra Montessori School offers short-term fees relief to families experiencing financial hardship. This program is designed to assist families who face temporary financial difficulties and need support with tuition costs.
description: Canberra Montessori School provides short-term fees relief as a form of financial assistance for families in financial hardship. The program does not offer formal scholarships to the general public but recognizes that families may experience temporary financial difficulties and provides support to help them maintain their child's enrolment. The specific financial value and duration of this relief is flexible and determined based on individual family circumstances. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the school directly to discuss their situation and explore available assistance options.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Need-based assessment on individual circumstances; Enrolment at Canberra Montessori School
special_conditions: Families must contact the school directly to apply. Eligibility is assessed on a case-by-case basis. Short-term relief is designed for temporary financial difficulties rather than ongoing support.

overview: Chapman Primary School operates an equity fund to assist families experiencing financial difficulty with the costs of schooling. The fund supports families to meet school-related expenses including uniforms, stationery, excursions, camps, and other school costs.
description: The Chapman Primary School Equity Fund is a financial assistance program designed to help families who are experiencing difficulty meeting the costs of schooling. The fund can be used to assist with a range of school expenses including school uniforms, stationery items, excursion costs, band participation costs, and other school-related expenses. Families experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to contact the school confidentially to discuss support with the Principal or Business Manager. The school has made every effort to keep costs to a minimal level and support is available on a confidential basis.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty with school costs; Open to all enrolled students; Confidential discussion with Principal or Business Manager required
special_conditions: Financial assistance is provided on a confidential basis. Families must speak directly with the Principal or Business Manager to discuss their situation. Support is available for book packs, uniforms, excursions, camps, band programs, and other school-related costs.

overview: The Principal's Scholarship of Excellence recognises outstanding applicants who demonstrate consistent excellence across multiple disciplines and activities. This award is offered to all-rounders who combine academic achievement with high achievement in sports, music, artistic pursuits or across other areas of school life.
description: The Principal's Scholarship of Excellence recognises exceptional all-round achievement across multiple disciplines and activities. Recipients demonstrate outstanding academic ability with mostly A grades across all subject areas, an exemplary work ethic, and consistent application to all pursuits. The scholarship provides 25% remission of tuition fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the Scholar's enrolment. Selection is based on a written assessment via Academic Assessment Services (AAS), and applicants may be means-tested as part of the process. The recipient must articulately express academic, career, personal and/or service and leadership goals, demonstrate significant leadership and/or service roles in their school or community, and show willingness to make a positive contribution to the overall life of the School. Renewal is conditional upon maintaining eligibility criteria and satisfactory academic and conduct standards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7–12; Outstanding academic ability with mostly A grades across all subject areas; Consistent record of excellence across multiple disciplines and activities; Evidence of exemplary work ethic and consistent application to all pursuits; Ability to articulate academic, career, personal and/or service and leadership goals; Eagerness to embrace the breadth of opportunities on offer at CGGS; Willingness and ability to make a positive and significant contribution to the overall life of the School; Potential and desire to lead, mentor and support peers at CGGS; Evidence of significant leadership and/or service roles in school or community; Willingness and ability to act as an ambassador for CGGS; Collaborative attitude towards peers and teachers; Active participation and achievement in school co-curricular programs
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered subject to annual review. School reserves the right to withdraw any Scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic or conduct standards. Recipients must maintain eligibility criteria and satisfactory progress throughout their enrolment.

overview: The Gabriel Foundation Scholarship (Academic) recognises outstanding academic achievement and dedication to learning. It is awarded to students who demonstrate exemplary work ethic, consistent application to all academic pursuits, and achievement at their academic potential in all areas of School life.
description: The Gabriel Foundation Scholarship (Academic) is awarded to students with exceptional academic achievement and unwavering commitment to learning. Recipients demonstrate an exemplary work ethic, consistent application to all academic pursuits, and consistent achievement at their academic potential in all areas of School life. They act as role models to others through striving for academic excellence. The scholarship provides 25% remission of tuition fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the Scholar's enrolment. There are four Gabriel Foundation Scholarships available annually. Selection involves written assessment via Academic Assessment Services (AAS) and applicants may request means-testing as part of the process. Recipients are expected to maintain academic standards and conduct throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7–12; Exemplary work ethic and consistent application to all academic pursuits; Consistent achievement at their academic potential in all areas of School life; Evidence of acting as a role model to others through striving for academic excellence; Shows evidence of a strong work ethic and consistent application to School-related pursuits; Consistently works towards and/or achieves at their potential; Has the ability to make a positive and significant contribution to the life of the School
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Four Gabriel Foundation Scholarships available annually. Subject to annual review. School reserves the right to withdraw any Scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress or conduct. Conditional upon recipient maintaining eligibility criteria and satisfactory academic standards throughout enrolment.

program_name: Scholarship Stream (Sport, Music, Arts and All-Rounder)
overview: The Scholarship Stream recognises and rewards students who have demonstrated academic excellence in combination with high achievement in sports, music, artistic pursuits, or across multiple disciplines. This is the School's primary stream for identifying talented students across various fields.
description: The Scholarship Stream specifically acknowledges and rewards students who have demonstrated academic excellence in combination with high achievement in sport, music, artistic pursuits, or across multiple disciplines. This stream also provides opportunities for Indigenous students, with boarding as an offering for students in remote, regional and rural communities. Scholarships are generally awarded at 25% remission of tuition and/or boarding fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the Scholar's enrolment. However, Scholarship applicants can request to have their applications means-tested as part of the process to potentially access higher levels of support. Selection requires applicants to sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test. Recipients are evaluated on their demonstrated academic excellence combined with evidence of high achievement in extracurricular pursuits, work ethic, and potential to contribute to the School community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7–12; Demonstrated academic excellence; Evidence of high achievement in sport, music, artistic pursuits or across multiple disciplines; For Indigenous students: appropriate identification and documentation; For boarding: residency in remote, regional or rural communities; Ability to make a positive and significant contribution to the life of the School; For boarders: ability and desire to make a positive and significant contribution to the Boarding House
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applicants can request means-testing to potentially access higher levels of support beyond standard 25% remission. Subject to annual review. Recipients must maintain academic standards and conduct throughout enrolment.

overview: The Bursary Stream specifically enables students who require financial tuition support to access a CGGS education. Bursaries are awarded based on demonstrated financial need and include opportunities for Indigenous students and those in remote, regional and rural communities.
description: The Bursary Stream is designed to enable students who require financial tuition support to access a CGGS education. This stream provides opportunities for Indigenous students, with boarding as an offering for students in remote, regional and rural communities. Bursaries provide at least 25% remission of tuition and/or boarding fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the scholar's enrolment. Bursaries can also be awarded to families in times of short-term hardships and are only granted when the School has means. This option requires a means-test via an independent external financial review conducted by the School's professional partner, Edstart, and a meeting with the Principal. Recipients must demonstrate school spirit through their work, conduct and participation in CGGS life, and demonstrate community spirit through service or leadership. Applications for the Bursary Program are accepted year-round.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7–12; Evidence of financial need requiring tuition support; For Indigenous students: appropriate identification and documentation; For boarding: residency in remote, regional or rural communities; Meets criteria for financial assistance via Edstart means-test; Embodies strong School spirit through work, conduct and participation in CGGS life; Demonstrates community spirit through service or leadership; Willing to undergo independent external financial review
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications accepted year-round. Requires independent external financial review by Edstart and meeting with Principal. Bursaries for short-term hardships only granted when the School has means. Subject to annual review and continuation of demonstrated financial need. Recipients must maintain good conduct and participation in School life.

overview: The Boarding Bursary provides financial assistance for students living in remote, regional and rural communities to access boarding facilities at CGGS. This program ensures rural and remote students can experience a CGGS education with boarding support.
description: The Boarding Bursary is specifically designed to provide financial assistance to students living in remote, regional and rural communities who require boarding facilities to attend CGGS. This bursary stream provides at least 25% remission of boarding fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the student's enrolment. Applications for Boarding Bursaries are accepted year-round. Selection requires a means-test via an independent external financial review conducted by the School's professional partner, Edstart, and a meeting with the Principal. Recipients must demonstrate strong School spirit through their work, conduct and participation in CGGS life, evidence of community spirit through service or leadership, and for boarders specifically, the ability and desire to make a positive and significant contribution to the Boarding House.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7–12; Residing in remote, regional or rural communities; Evidence of financial need requiring boarding support; Meets criteria for financial assistance via Edstart means-test; Embodies strong School spirit through work, conduct and participation in CGGS life; Demonstrates community spirit through service or leadership; For boarders: ability and desire to make a positive contribution to the Boarding House; Willing to undergo independent external financial review
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications accepted year-round. Specifically for students in remote, regional and rural communities. Requires independent external financial review by Edstart and meeting with Principal. Subject to annual review and continuation of demonstrated financial need. Recipients must maintain good conduct and boarding house responsibilities.

school_name: Canberra Girls Grammar School - Junior School
overview: Canberra Girls Grammar School offers the Gabriel Foundation Scholarship (Academic) to reward outstanding achievement in students demonstrating academic excellence. The scholarship recognises students who exhibit exemplary work ethic and consistent application to all academic pursuits whilst serving as role models through striving for academic excellence.
description: The Gabriel Foundation Scholarship (Academic) is awarded to students who demonstrate exemplary work ethic and consistent application to academic pursuits. Recipients are expected to consistently work towards and achieve at their academic potential in all areas of school life and act as role models through striving for academic excellence. The scholarship provides 25% fee remission of tuition fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the scholar's enrolment. Selection requires sitting the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test. Scholarship applicants may request to have their applications means-tested as part of the process. Recipients must maintain consistent academic performance and continue to demonstrate the values that define the scholarship throughout their tenure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 to Year 11; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test; Applicants are permanent Australian Residents or hold Australian Citizenship; Demonstrating exemplary work ethic and consistent application to academic pursuits; Consistently works towards and/or achieves at their academic potential; Acts as a role model to others through striving for academic excellence
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Generally, scholarships are awarded at 25%, however, scholarship applicants can request to have their applications means-tested as part of the process. The Gabriel Foundation has made a CGGS education possible for many students since its inception, as it funds both the Scholarship and Bursary offerings at the School.

school_name: Canberra Girls Grammar School - Junior School
overview: Canberra Girls Grammar School offers the Gabriel Foundation Scholarship (Arts) to recognise and reward students demonstrating excellence in artistic pursuits. The scholarship acknowledges students who have combined academic excellence with exceptional achievement in creative and performing arts.
description: The Gabriel Foundation Scholarship (Arts) is awarded to students who demonstrate excellence in artistic pursuits and maintain a strong work ethic applied consistently to arts-related activities. Recipients must work towards and achieve at their potential in all areas of school life and commit to giving freely of their time to creative and performing arts events and activities as required, as well as broader school events and activities. The scholarship provides 25% fee remission of tuition fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the scholar's enrolment. Selection requires sitting the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test and demonstrating evidence of outstanding artistic achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 to Year 11; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test; Applicants are permanent Australian Residents or hold Australian Citizenship; Demonstrates excellence in The Arts; Shows exemplary work ethic and consistent application to artistic pursuits; Commits to participate in creative/performing arts events and activities; Consistently works towards and/or achieves at their potential in all areas of school life
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Recipients must commit to give freely of their time to all creative/performing arts events and activities as required, as well as broader School events and activities. Generally, scholarships are awarded at 25%, however, scholarship applicants can request to have their applications means-tested as part of the process.

school_name: Canberra Girls Grammar School - Junior School
overview: Canberra Girls Grammar School offers the Gabriel Foundation Scholarship (Music) to recognise and support students demonstrating exceptional musical achievement. The scholarship rewards students who combine strong academic performance with outstanding musical ability and commitment to the school's music program.
description: The Gabriel Foundation Scholarship (Music) is awarded to students who demonstrate exemplary work ethic and consistent application to musical pursuits. Recipients must work towards and achieve at their potential in all areas of school life, particularly in Music, and are required to commit time to the Music Academy events and activities as well as broader school events and activities. The scholarship provides 25% fee remission of tuition fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the scholar's enrolment. Selection requires sitting the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test and demonstrating substantial musical achievement through audition and portfolio assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 to Year 11; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test; Applicants are permanent Australian Residents or hold Australian Citizenship; Demonstrates excellence in musical pursuits; Shows exemplary work ethic and consistent application to music studies; Commits to participate in Music Academy and broader school events; Consistently works towards and/or achieves at their potential in all areas of school life
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Recipients must commit to give freely of their time to all Music Academy events and activities as well as broader School events and activities. Generally, scholarships are awarded at 25%, however, scholarship applicants can request to have their applications means-tested as part of the process.

school_name: Canberra Girls Grammar School - Junior School
overview: Canberra Girls Grammar School offers the Principal's Scholarship of Excellence to recognise outstanding all-rounder students. The scholarship awards students who demonstrate exceptional excellence across multiple disciplines and activities, combining outstanding academic ability with high achievement in co-curricular pursuits.
description: The Principal's Scholarship of Excellence recognises students who are considered outstanding applicants in multiple fields, demonstrating a consistent record of excellence across multiple disciplines and activities. Recipients must exhibit outstanding academic ability (mostly A grades across all subject areas) and show evidence of exemplary work ethic with consistent application to all pursuits. Recipients should articulate clear academic, career, personal and/or service and leadership goals, and demonstrate eagerness to embrace the breadth of opportunities offered at CGGS. The scholarship provides at least 25% fee remission of tuition and/or boarding fees from Years 7–12, or for the remainder of the scholar's enrolment. Selection requires sitting the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 to Year 11; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) test; Applicants are permanent Australian Residents or hold Australian Citizenship; Consistent record of excellence across multiple disciplines and activities; Outstanding academic ability (mostly A grades across all subject areas); Evidence of exemplary work ethic and consistent application to all pursuits; Ability to articulate academic, career, personal and/or service and leadership goals; Eagerness to embrace breadth of opportunities at CGGS
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship applicants can request to have their applications means-tested as part of the process. Recipients should demonstrate the ability to make a positive and significant contribution to the life of the school. For boarders, recipients must demonstrate the ability and desire to make a positive and significant contribution to the Boarding House.

school_name: Canberra Girls Grammar School - Junior School
overview: Canberra Girls Grammar School offers the Bursary Program to provide financial tuition support for students whose families require assistance. The bursary recognises that access to education should not be limited by financial circumstances and aims to ensure students from diverse backgrounds can access a world-class CGGS education.
description: The CGGS Bursary Program specifically enables students who require financial tuition support to access the school's education. This stream provides opportunities for Indigenous students, with boarding as an offering for students in remote, regional and rural communities. Bursaries can also be awarded to families in times of short-term hardship and are only granted when the School has means. This option requires a means-test via an independent external financial review conducted by the School's professional partner, Edstart, and a meeting with the Principal. Bursary remission is partial and is typically determined on an individual basis depending on assessed financial need and school capacity. Applications for the Bursary Program are accepted year-round.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 to Year 11; Applicants are permanent Australian Residents or hold Australian Citizenship; Students require financial tuition support; Must complete independent financial review with Edstart; Must meet with the Principal; Shows evidence of strong work ethic and consistent application to school-related pursuits; Consistently works towards and/or achieves at their potential; Has ability to make positive and significant contribution to school life
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Bursaries can also be awarded to families in times of short-term hardship and are only granted when the School has means. This bursary stream provides opportunities for Indigenous students, with boarding as an offering for students in remote, regional and rural communities. Bursary recipients may also request to have applications considered in combination with scholarship criteria. For boarders, applicants must have the ability and desire to make a positive and significant contribution to the Boarding House.

school_name: Canberra Girls Grammar School - Junior School
overview: Canberra Girls Grammar School offers the Archer Family Bursary for Year 11 students requiring financial support. The bursary is named in honour of the Archer family and provides financial assistance to support students through their senior studies.
description: The Archer Family Bursary is an additional option in the Bursary Program specifically for Year 11 students, supporting the duration of their senior studies through Years 11 and 12. The Bursary is named in honour of Mr Keith Archer, a valued member of the CGGS Board, Parents & Friends Association, and the Gabriel Foundation, and his daughter, Mrs Margaret Cornwell. A recipient of this award must meet the criteria for financial assistance and must embody strong school spirit through their work, conduct and participation in CGGS life. Additionally, recipients must demonstrate community spirit through service or leadership. The bursary provides partial remission of tuition and/or boarding fees for Years 11–12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 only; Applicants are permanent Australian Residents or hold Australian Citizenship; Must meet financial assistance criteria; Embodies strong school spirit through work, conduct and participation in CGGS life; Demonstrates community spirit through service or leadership; Must be able to make a positive and significant contribution to school life
special_conditions: Specifically for Year 11 entry, supporting the duration of senior studies. Recipients must embody strong school spirit through work, conduct and participation in CGGS life, and must demonstrate community spirit through service or leadership. This bursary recognises the legacy of the Archer family's generous support of CGGS and the Gabriel Foundation.

overview: Daramalan College offers the Scholarship Fund to provide financial assistance to eligible families experiencing financial hardship that would otherwise prevent their child from attending the College. The fund has supported an average of five students each year since its establishment in 2013.
description: The Daramalan College Scholarship Fund was established in 2013 and aims to offer scholarships to several students each year with the long-term goal of supporting scholarship recipients through a self-sustaining fund. Since 1962, Daramalan College has been providing financial assistance to eligible families whose situation would exclude them from giving their child an MSC (Missionaries of the Sacred Heart) education. The scholarship helps ensure students can continue their education by covering part or all of their tuition fees and providing essential resources, such as a laptop, to support their learning. Applications open at the beginning of Term 3 each year, providing financial assistance to students whose families are experiencing significant financial need. The financial benefit varies and may include partial or full tuition fee remission depending on individual circumstances and available funds.
eligibility_criteria: Current students experiencing significant financial hardship; Families whose financial circumstances would otherwise exclude them from attending Daramalan College; Students enrolled or planning to enrol at the College
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must be current students experiencing financial hardship. The fund aims for sustainability and support is offered on an annual basis subject to fund availability. Scholarships may cover tuition fees and provide essential learning resources such as laptops.

overview: Dickson College students are eligible for the ACT Public Education Scholarship, which recognises achievement in academic, creative and sporting pursuits, combined with contribution to the college community. The scholarship is sponsored by Capital Chemist and Friends of Zainab.
description: The ACT Public Education Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate excellence across multiple areas of achievement. Recipients are recognised for their academic performance, creative talents, and sporting abilities, as well as their positive contribution and engagement within the Dickson College community. This externally-sponsored scholarship acknowledges well-rounded students who embody the college's values of excellence, opportunity and community. The scholarship is administered by Capital Chemist and Friends of Zainab. Specific financial value, number awarded per year, and application procedures are not publicly detailed on the college website.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled at Dickson College; Demonstrated achievement in academic performance; Demonstrated achievement in creative pursuits; Demonstrated achievement in sporting pursuits; Active contribution to Dickson College community
special_conditions: External scholarship sponsored by Capital Chemist and Friends of Zainab. Specific renewal conditions and selection criteria not publicly specified.

program_name: Secondary and Tertiary Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship Program
overview: This is an ACT Government initiative available to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students at Dickson College, designed to increase retention and Year 12 completion rates. The program recognises and supports First Nations students' achievement and contribution to their educational and broader communities.
description: The Secondary and Tertiary Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship Program is an ACT Government initiative that supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in their secondary education. The program aims to increase retention and Year 12 attainment, increase the number of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander teachers, and increase the number of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people with tertiary qualifications. Dickson College students are eligible to apply for this recognition and support. Recipients are acknowledged for their academic performance, community engagement, and wellbeing achievements. The specific financial value and application timeline are not published on the Dickson College website.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled at Dickson College; Identified as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander; Meet academic and community contribution criteria as determined by ACT Government
special_conditions: ACT Government-administered program. Recipients must demonstrate academic excellence alongside community involvement and wellbeing pursuits. Scholarship recipients are publicly recognised at formal ceremonies throughout the ACT.

school_name: Melba Copland Secondary School Copland Campus Years 7 - 9
Melba Copland Secondary School Copland Campus Years 10 - 12

overview: Narrabundah College offers Music Scholarships to students demonstrating excellence in music performance. The scholarship recognises talented musicians and provides opportunities to develop their musical gifts.
description: Narrabundah College offers Music Scholarships through a competitive audition process. Auditions are held in March each year. The scholarship is available to Year 11 and Year 12 students entering the college. Selection is based on demonstrated musical performance ability through live audition. Specific financial value, duration, and renewal conditions are not publicly detailed on the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12; Must audition in March; Demonstrated musical performance ability required
special_conditions: Auditions held annually in March. Specific conditions regarding renewal, minimum performance standards, and co-curricular music participation are not publicly documented.

program_name: AVCAT Scholarships (Long Tan Bursaries and Sponsored Scholarships)
overview: Orana Steiner School lists AVCAT (Australian Veterans' Children Assistance Trust) scholarships for eligible tertiary education students. The program provides financial support to children and grandchildren of ex-serving Australian veterans through both sponsored scholarships and Long Tan Bursaries.
description: AVCAT Scholarships are tertiary education programs managed through the Australian Veterans' Children Assistance Trust. All bursaries and sponsored scholarships provide $4,000–$6,000 per year for three years to full-time students enrolled in a minimum one-year course at university, TAFE or RTO. Sponsored scholarships are for children and grandchildren of ex-serving veterans, whilst Long Tan Bursaries are specifically for children and grandchildren of Vietnam Veterans. The Long Tan Bursary is funded by the Australian Government Department of Veterans' Affairs. Scholarships are awarded based on financial need and academic potential.
eligibility_criteria: Must be child, stepchild, foster child, or grandchild of an ex-serving Australian veteran; Must be enrolled in a minimum one-year tertiary course at university, TAFE or RTO; Assessed on financial need and academic potential
special_conditions: This is a tertiary education scholarship, not for primary or secondary school students. Applications were last updated in 2022.

overview: Sacred Heart Primary School Pearce offers available fee concessions to assist families with school fees. Parents are encouraged to enquire with the school directly to learn more about the specific financial assistance options available.
description: Sacred Heart Primary School Pearce recognises the financial demands placed on household budgets and has implemented a fee concessions program to support families in need. The school offers flexible payment options for school fees, allowing families to arrange payments on a termly, monthly, fortnightly or weekly basis. Families experiencing financial difficulty are welcome to contact the school directly to discuss available fee concessions and arrange a payment plan that suits their circumstances. The school's commitment is to ensure that no student is disadvantaged due to their family's financial situation. Specific financial amounts and conditions are determined on a case-by-case basis through direct negotiation with the school administration.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all enrolled families; Demonstrated financial need; Must contact school directly for enquiry and assessment
special_conditions: Fee concessions are negotiated on a case-by-case basis. Families must contact the school office to discuss their circumstances and available options. The school supports flexible payment arrangements including termly, monthly, fortnightly or weekly payments.

overview: Radford College, in partnership with the Boorer Foundation, offers a life-changing scholarship opportunity for one student commencing Year 7. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding potential and removes financial barriers to accessing a Radford education.
description: The Boorer Foundation Scholarship is a full scholarship for one Year 7 student commencing in 2027. The scholarship covers 100% of tuition and capital levy fees, plus up to 100% of other college-related fees for the duration of enrolment at the school. Applicants must demonstrate outstanding achievement and community involvement, but whose financial circumstances prevent them from attending the college. The selection process includes an initial application form submission, and only shortlisted applicants will be required to complete a detailed financial statement, provide referees, and participate in an interview with a panel. Australian citizenship or permanent residency visa is required.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2027; Must demonstrate outstanding achievement and community involvement; Must have financial need preventing attendance at the college; Australian citizenship or permanent residency visa required; Must support college Christian ethos and policies
special_conditions: Applications close on Friday, 27 March 2026. Late applications will not be accepted. Only shortlisted applicants will be required to complete detailed financial statement, provide referees and be interviewed by a panel. The successful applicant will be selected in time for them to take their place in Year 7 orientation. Radford College does not have registration to enrol international/overseas students. Australian citizenship or permanent residency visa required.

overview: Radford College's Foundation Senior Scholarship is a full, two-year scholarship for one student commencing Year 11. This scholarship recognises outstanding achievement and community involvement while removing financial barriers for students unable to attend the college.
description: The Foundation Senior Scholarship is a comprehensive two-year scholarship covering 100% of tuition and capital levy fees for Years 11 and 12, plus up to 100% of other college-related fees. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding achievement and community involvement, but whose financial circumstances prevent them from attending Radford College. The selection process requires applicants to submit an initial application form, after which only shortlisted candidates are invited to complete a detailed financial statement, provide referees, and participate in an interview with a selection panel. The successful applicant will remain anonymous during their time at Radford and will be selected in time to participate in Year 11 orientation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11; Must be currently enrolled at another school; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and community involvement; Must have financial circumstances preventing attendance; Passionate about pursuing higher education; Support of college Christian ethos required; Australian citizenship or permanent residency visa required
special_conditions: Applications close on Friday, 27 March 2026. Late applications will not be accepted. Only shortlisted applicants required to complete detailed financial statement, provide referees and be interviewed. Successful applicant remains anonymous during time at Radford. Selected in time for Year 11 orientation. Scholarship covers two years (Years 11 and 12). Radford College does not have registration to enrol international/overseas students.

overview: St Edmund's College offers the Senior School Academic Scholarship to Year 10 students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and wish to continue to Year 11 and 12. The scholarship recognises students who excel throughout their secondary studies and contribute positively to the College's academic and pastoral life.
description: The Senior School Academic Scholarship is awarded to Year 10 students returning to St Edmund's College for Years 11 and 12 on the basis of their academic performance throughout Year 10, participation in College life, and contribution to the ethos of St Edmund's College. The scholarship is awarded for both years of senior study (Years 11 and 12) conditional upon students maintaining high levels of academic achievement and not engaging in behaviour contrary to Edmund Rice School values. Selection is based on a panel review of all applications, with successful applicants notified separately. The specific fee reduction or value is not published on the official page but is determined at the College's discretion. All applicants will be informed of the outcome regardless of scholarship award status.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 10 at St Edmund's College; Intending to return for Year 11 and 12; Demonstrated high academic performance throughout Year 10; Active participation in College life; Alignment with Edmund Rice School values
special_conditions: Award is granted for both years of senior study on the condition that students maintain high levels of academic achievement in Years 11 and 12 and do not engage in any behaviour that is contrary to the values of an Edmund Rice School. A panel will review all applications.

program_name: St Edmund's College Foundation Financial Assistance
overview: The St Edmund's College Foundation provides financial assistance to marginalised or disadvantaged families to alleviate the cost of educating their sons. Inspired by the charism of Edmund Rice, this foundation-supported program reflects the College's commitment to ensuring access to Catholic education regardless of financial circumstance.
description: The St Edmund's College Foundation was established to give financial assistance to marginalised or disadvantaged families so that the cost of educating their sons at the College would be alleviated. This program reflects the Edmund Rice tradition of supporting families facing financial hardship. The Foundation operates as a separate entity offering need-based support. Specific fee reduction amounts, application processes, and timelines are not detailed on the main College website. Families seeking assistance are encouraged to contact the College directly to discuss eligibility and available support options.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship or disadvantage; Seeking to access Catholic education for their sons; All year levels considered
special_conditions: Applications must demonstrate genuine financial hardship or disadvantage. The Foundation operates at the College's discretion in providing assistance.

school_name: St Clare of Assisi Primary School
St John the Apostle Primary School
St Francis of Assisi Primary School

overview: St Clare's College offers the Junior Leadership Scholarship to support students who demonstrate strong leadership qualities and contribute to the college community. The scholarship recognises junior students who display excellent behaviour and provide positive support to their peers.
description: The Junior Leadership Scholarship is awarded to current and prospective junior students who meet the selection criteria. Applicants must have an excellent record of behaviour and support the College's Positive Behaviour (Personal Best) policy. Recipients must demonstrate leadership and support to other students. Failure to meet the College's standard of behaviour may jeopardise the recipient's scholarship. All scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal. Applications must be submitted by 31 March in the year prior to the scholarship year being applied for. The financial benefit and duration of this scholarship are not specified on the available page.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective junior students; Excellent record of behaviour; Support for College's Positive Behaviour (Personal Best) policy; Demonstrated leadership; Support other students; Applications submitted by 31 March in year prior to scholarship year
special_conditions: All scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal. Failure to meet the College's standard of behaviour may jeopardise the recipient's scholarship. Supporting documentation must be received by the College by 31 March in the year prior to the scholarship year that you are applying for.

overview: St Clare's College offers the Senior Leadership Scholarship to senior students who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities and commitment to supporting the college community. The scholarship recognises Year 11 and Year 12 students who display excellent behaviour and contribute positively to peer support.
description: The Senior Leadership Scholarship is awarded to current and prospective senior students who meet the specified selection criteria. Applicants must maintain an excellent record of behaviour and support the College's Positive Behaviour (Personal Best) policy. Recipients must demonstrate leadership and actively support other students. Failure to meet the College's standard of behaviour may jeopardise the recipient's scholarship. All scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal. Applications must be submitted by 31 March in the year prior to the scholarship year being applied for. The exact financial benefit and duration of this scholarship are not specified on the available page.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective senior students; Excellent record of behaviour; Support for College's Positive Behaviour (Personal Best) policy; Demonstrated leadership; Support other students; Applications submitted by 31 March in year prior to scholarship year
special_conditions: All scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal. Failure to meet the College's standard of behaviour may jeopardise the recipient's scholarship. Supporting documentation must be received by the College by 31 March in the year prior to the scholarship year that you are applying for.

school_name: St Mary MacKillop College - Wanniassa Campus

overview: St Jude's Primary School offers the Music and Arts Scholarship to honour the legacy of Josh de Smet, a beloved music teacher who passed away in 2005. The scholarship annually recognises Year 2 students who demonstrate genuine interest and commitment to music and the arts, supporting their continued development in these areas.
description: The Music and Arts Scholarship is an annual award established in 2005 to perpetuate the memory of Josh de Smet, who taught music to all Infants grades at St Jude's Primary School. The scholarship provides financial support valued at approximately $400 (as of 2024) that can be used to pay for music instrument lessons, singing lessons, or other arts-related educational expenses. The award is decided by a sub-committee of the School Community Council and school staff based on a two-part application process, with Part 1 completed by parents or guardians at home and Part 2 completed by the student at school under teacher supervision. Selection criteria include demonstrated previous interest in the arts through activities such as playing an instrument, displaying interest in school music, participating in school choirs, or involvement in other arts areas, combined with an intention to further develop skills and interests. Recipients must commit to writing to the School Community Council within the following year to outline how the money has helped their music or art development.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 2 student enrolled at St Jude's Primary School for the next academic year; Previous interest in the Arts - for example, already plays an instrument, displays an interest in music at school, participates in school choirs, or displays an interest in other areas of the Arts; Displays an intention to further develop skills and interest in chosen area
special_conditions: The money for the scholarship comes initially from a foundation fund established in 2005 and shall be added to by the School Community Council each year. The award winner is asked to write to the School Community Council in the following year after receiving the award, outlining how the money has helped their music or art development. Applications need to be handed in to the front office usually by around week 6 of Term 4.

overview: St John Paul II College offers scholarships to Year 11 students who face financial constraints preventing access to Years 11 and 12 studies. These scholarships recognise students whose families require financial support to continue their Catholic education at JPC.
description: Full and Part Scholarships are offered to Year 11 students for Years 11 and 12 to support families experiencing financial hardship. Recipients of these awards are kept confidential. The scholarship is designed to ensure that no student is denied a Catholic education at JPC based on financial inability. Scholarships provide support for the duration of Years 11 and 12 at the College. Applicants must demonstrate genuine financial need and commitment to the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 students; Students continuing to Year 11 and Year 12; Families experiencing financial constraints; Demonstrated commitment to College values of Justice, Peace and Courage
special_conditions: Recipients of these awards will be kept confidential. Scholarships are subject to annual review. Students must maintain commitment to College values and demonstrate hard work and contribution to College life.

overview: St John Paul II College offers Academic Scholarships to Year 11 students who demonstrate strong academic achievement. These scholarships recognise students who have achieved excellence in their studies while contributing positively to College life.
description: Academic scholarships are awarded to Year 11 students who demonstrate excellence in their academic work. Recipients must be hard working students who can make a real contribution to the life and culture of the College and have demonstrated the core values of Justice, Peace and Courage. Applicants are required to complete an application form detailing their academic contributions to date and their plans for future contributions to the College community. Scholarships provide support for Years 11 and 12 and are subject to annual review of academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 students; Demonstrated academic excellence; Hard working students; Demonstrated commitment to College core values of Justice, Peace and Courage; Active contribution to College life and culture
special_conditions: Applicants must complete an application form detailing contributions to date and contributions they plan to make to the College in the future. Scholarships are subject to annual review. Full and Part Scholarships available for Years 11 and 12.

overview: St John Paul II College provides fee concessions to enrolled families experiencing financial hardship. In accordance with Archdiocesan policy, the College ensures that no child is denied Catholic schooling due to inability to pay.
description: St John Paul II College offers the opportunity to apply for fee concessions to current, enrolled families finding themselves in situations of financial hardship. Concession applications are financially means-tested and reviewed annually. The Archdiocesan policy states that no child is to be denied Catholic schooling simply because of an inability (as opposed to unwillingness) of parents or carers to meet financial requirements. Eligible families should contact the College finance office to request an application form and receive more information about the process.
eligibility_criteria: Current enrolled families; Experiencing financial hardship; Willing to complete financial means-test; Reviewed annually for continued eligibility
special_conditions: Applications are financially means-tested and reviewed annually. Families should contact jpc.finance@cg.catholic.edu.au to request an application form. This is a separate program from formal scholarships.

overview: St Monica's Primary School offers fee assistance to families experiencing significant financial difficulties. The program is designed to support families during periods of extreme hardship and is administered in accordance with Catholic Education Commission Goulburn policy ensuring no child is denied Catholic education due to inability to pay fees.
description: The Fee Assistance Program provides families experiencing significant financial difficulties with fee reductions to support them during extreme hardship. The financial benefit varies based on individual circumstances and assessment of the family's financial situation. Assistance is typically provided for a short period to help families through temporary hardship periods. The application process requires families to complete a Fee Assistance Application Form and submit supporting financial documentation. All reductions granted apply on a year-by-year basis, meaning families must submit a separate application each year if they require ongoing assistance. Families should contact Kirralea Thompson, Finance Manager, to obtain the necessary application form and discuss their circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing significant financial difficulties; Must demonstrate genuine inability to pay school fees; Separate application required each year
special_conditions: Fee assistance is usually for a short period of time to support families during extreme hardship. Any reductions granted apply on a year-by-year basis, meaning a separate application is required each year. It is Catholic Education Commission Goulburn policy that no child shall be denied Catholic schooling simply because of an inability to pay school fees.

overview: St Monica's Primary School offers flexible payment arrangement options to help families manage school fees. Families may choose to pay fees in various instalments to accommodate their financial circumstances and budgeting needs.
description: St Monica's Primary School provides flexible payment options for school fees to assist families in managing their financial obligations. Families are welcome to set up scheduled payments either termly, monthly, fortnightly or weekly according to their preference and capacity. Fees and levies may be paid in full each term or by instalments as is most convenient for families. Payment can be made using BPAY, EFTPOS facilities at the School Office, by telephone for credit cards, or by Direct Debit BPoint. This non-monetary support program helps remove barriers to enrolment by allowing families to spread fee payments throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all enrolled families; Must maintain regular payment arrangements; Available to all year levels
special_conditions: Families must maintain their agreed payment schedule. All occasions of leave of absence from school attendance must be paid in full unless the child ceases to be enrolled at the school. Extended periods of absence such as overseas holidays require payment of full fees for that period.

overview: St Thomas More's Primary School offers fee concession assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school follows the Catholic Education Commission Archdiocese (CECG) policy that no child is denied Catholic education because of inability to pay fees. Families may apply for fee concessions to reduce their financial obligations.
description: The Fee Concession Program provides financial assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship who are unable, for reasonable causes such as illness or unemployment, to meet their full school fee obligations. Families must initiate early communication with the school by completing a Fee Concession Application form and requesting an interview with school staff. The working definition of genuine financial hardship encompasses situations where families cannot reasonably discharge their financial obligations due to changed circumstances, as opposed to unwillingness to pay. Financial hardship may be of limited or long-term duration. The school treats all applications with absolute discretion and confidentiality. According to CECG policy, no student will be denied a Catholic education because of a family's genuine financial hardship, and appropriate fee concession arrangements will be made where justified. The concession must be renewed annually with a new application form submitted each year.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; Unable to pay school fees due to illness, unemployment or other reasonable cause; Not due to unwillingness or reluctance to pay; Must initiate early communication with school; Must complete Fee Concession Application form; Must participate in interview with school staff
special_conditions: It is CECG policy that no child is to be denied Catholic schooling simply because of an inability (as opposed to an unwillingness) of a parent/carer to meet financial requirements. Concession arrangements must be renewed annually; a new Fee Concession Application will be required for each new year. All applications treated with absolute discretion and confidentiality. Families are welcome to set up scheduled payments either termly, monthly, fortnightly or weekly.

school_name: St Thomas the Apostle Primary School
overview: St Thomas the Apostle Primary School provides fee concessions and financial assistance through the Catholic Education Canberra and Goulburn (CECG) archdiocese policy. The school ensures that no child is denied Catholic schooling due to an inability to meet financial requirements.
description: The Catholic Education Canberra and Goulburn archdiocese has a formal policy stating that no child is to be denied Catholic schooling simply because of an inability (as opposed to an unwillingness) of a parent or carer to meet financial requirements. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the school to enquire about available fee concessions. Fee concessions are assessed on a case-by-case basis and treated with dignity, fairness, compassion and confidentiality. Families are welcome to set up scheduled payments either termly, monthly, fortnightly or weekly to assist with affordability. The Catholic Education Commission is responsible for setting the tuition and building levy each year. If families need any assistance, they should contact the Front Office directly to discuss options.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship or inability to pay full fees; All year levels (Kindergarten to Year 6); Assessment based on individual family circumstances; Not conditional on Catholic faith background
special_conditions: Requests for fee concessions are treated with dignity, fairness, compassion and confidentiality. Enquiries should be made directly with the Front Office. Families with 4 or more children at the school are encouraged to enquire with the Principal about additional discounts available beyond the standard fee concession program.

school_name: Sts Peter and Paul Primary School

overview: Taqwa School offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. The concessions are applied to tuition fees and are assessed based on individual family circumstances and student contribution to the school community.
description: Taqwa School provides fee concessions ranging from 10% to 75% of the full cost of tuition fees for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. Applications are assessed according to need and are subject to the student's contribution to the class and their family's contribution to the school. Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year, after which a new application is required for the subsequent 12-month period. The concessions are only applied to tuition fees and do not cover other charges such as admission fees, books and stationery, excursions and outdoor activities, or ICT levy. A compassionate and just approach under the ethos and values of Taqwa School is used when reviewing applications. Supporting documents may be requested to attach to the application form.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must demonstrate genuine financial hardship; Student must be enrolled or seeking enrolment at Taqwa School; Application may be made at the commencement of each year or at any other time in exceptional cases; Concessions valid for maximum 12 months within a calendar year
special_conditions: Concessions are issued for a maximum period of 12 months within a calendar year. Consideration for the subsequent 12-month period will require a new application. Concessions are only applied to tuition fees and do not include admission fees, books and stationery charges, excursions and outdoor activities charges, or ICT levy. Concession application forms are available from the school office. Parents may be requested to supply supporting documents to attach to their application.

school_name: University of Canberra High School Kaleen
overview: UC High School Kaleen offers the Beyond Gifted and Talented Program for students who are identified as gifted and/or talented. The program provides structure and opportunities for enrichment both within and outside of the classroom for identified students, as well as enrichment opportunities accessible to all students across the school.
description: The Beyond program at UC High School Kaleen is designed to recognise and support gifted and talented students. The program uses a range of data to identify students, including academic results, external standardised tests, PAT testing, external competition results, teacher and parent nomination, and external testing provided by families. Students can be referred to Beyond at any time throughout their time at UCHSK. The program provides differentiated curriculum and assessment opportunities with a focus on one or two Key Learning Areas (KLAs) for each student. Enrichment opportunities such as ethics, philosophy and psychology classes are provided to complement learning in the KLAs. External opportunities such as competitions, excursions and guest speakers are also available to both Beyond students and all other students in the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at UC High School Kaleen; Identification through academic results, standardised tests, PAT testing, external competition results, teacher nomination, parent nomination, or external testing; Can be referred at any time during enrolment at UCHSK
special_conditions: The program is based on the principles of Enrichment and Inclusion. All enrichment opportunities can be accessed by students across the school.

overview: Turner School provides gifted and talented education to identified students through planned curriculum differentiation, mentoring, enrichment opportunities, and special interest projects. The school operates an inclusive model allowing flexibility in provision of programs to cater for the interests, strengths and needs of gifted students.
description: Turner School's Gifted and Talented Education program provides curriculum differentiation both within regular classroom settings and through additional learning opportunities. Students identified as gifted and talented participate in enrichment groups, mentoring relationships with specialists in their areas of giftedness, and targeted activities linked to classroom curriculum. The school operates withdrawal programs in Terms 2 and 3 where selected students in Years 1-6 participate in weekly enrichment activities. Special interest projects, festivals, challenges and competitions complement classroom provision. The program includes participation in external activities such as the Tournament of Minds problem-solving challenge. Individual Learning Plans (ILPs) are established for identified gifted students in consultation with families, teachers and support staff to ensure appropriate challenge and extension.
eligibility_criteria: Students identified through a diagnostic process that locates and describes specific or multiple gifts; Selection process conducted by school staff in consultation with families; Open to all year levels from Year 1 to Year 6; No residency restrictions beyond school enrolment
special_conditions: Identification is an ongoing diagnostic process. Students participate in consultation with families regarding educational programs and plans. The school employs a Gifted and Talented Liaison Officer who works with staff and families to coordinate support. Provision includes mentoring by school staff and external specialists in areas of identified giftedness. Participation is flexible and can involve withdrawal from regular classes for targeted enrichment activities.

overview: Turner School offers Senior Storytelling Awards to recognize and celebrate outstanding achievement in storytelling and creative writing among Year 3-6 students. The awards form part of the school's home learning program and provide opportunities for students to develop and showcase their literacy skills.
description: The Senior Storytelling Awards are recognition awards presented to students in Years 3-6 who demonstrate excellence in storytelling and creative writing. These awards are integrated into the school's home learning program and provide high investment learning experiences for senior students. Students have the opportunity to participate in this activity across multiple terms, with awards recognizing outstanding contributions. The awards celebrate student creativity, imagination and mastery of narrative techniques. This is a non-monetary recognition award that acknowledges student achievement in a specific literacy domain and encourages continued development of writing skills across the primary years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 3-6; Must be enrolled at Turner School; Participation is through home learning opportunities integrated across the school term
special_conditions: Awards are presented through school assemblies and celebrations. Participation is encouraged through home learning grids provided at the start of each term. Recognition is based on quality of storytelling and creative writing demonstrated.

overview: Turner School accepts voluntary contributions from families to support the school's educational programs and resources. These optional contributions help fund additional resources and equipment across the school from Preschool to Year 6. Families experiencing financial hardship are not disadvantaged as contributions are entirely voluntary.
description: Turner School operates a voluntary contributions system where families are invited to contribute financially to support school operations and enhancements. Contributions can be made through multiple payment methods including bank transfer, QuickWeb (credit card), cash or cheque. Families may also make tax-deductible donations to the Library Fund to support library resources and materials. Contributions are genuinely voluntary and no student is disadvantaged by non-payment. The contributions support general school resources, equipment, library materials and co-curricular activities. As a government school in the ACT, tuition is free and compulsory curriculum resources are provided at no cost. All voluntary contributions are set by the School Board in consultation with the Parent and Community (P&C) association annually.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all enrolled families at Turner School; Contributions are entirely voluntary; No student is disadvantaged by non-payment; Payment methods include bank transfer, QuickWeb, cash and cheque; Tax-deductible donations available for Library Fund contributions
special_conditions: Contributions are voluntary and families are not obligated to pay. School must not deny any student access to curriculum or educational opportunities based on non-payment of contributions. As per ACT government school policy, all essential curriculum resources are provided free of charge. Voluntary contributions support enhanced resources and equipment. Privacy and dignity of families experiencing hardship are maintained.

school_name: University of Canberra Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra
overview: UC Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra offers the Academic Excellence Program to support students striving to excel in their intellectual, creative or artistic capacity, or in specific academic fields. The program provides targeted mentorship and opportunities in Years 11 and 12 for students demonstrating exceptional academic ability.
description: The Academic Excellence Program is designed for students with motivation and a growth mindset who aim to achieve excellence. Entry is determined through a combination of application, aptitude testing, and referee reports from nominated teachers. Upon successful entry, opportunities are designed through consultation with the Academic Excellence Team to support students in their academic pursuits while developing core skills for success beyond the ACT Year 12 Certificate. Students within the program are expected to engage in extracurricular activities across the school proactively and attend targeted Academic Excellence events. The program includes enrichment opportunities through Global Classroom, which enables students to connect with a cohort from diverse backgrounds and develop a global perspective by engaging with other cultures and differing educational approaches.
eligibility_criteria: Students with demonstrated motivation and growth mindset; Selection through application, aptitude testing, and teacher referee reports; Open to Years 11 and 12 students
special_conditions: Entry determined through combination of application, aptitude testing and referee reports. Students expected to engage in extracurricular activities and attend targeted Academic Excellence events.

school_name: University of Canberra Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra
overview: UC Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra offers the Elite Sports Program to support local elite student athletes balancing high-performance training with academic studies. The program provides specialized support and athlete development with links to the Australian Institute of Sport.
description: The Elite Sports Program is designed for local students who are elite athletes pursuing high-performance training while completing Years 11 and 12. Students in the program meet twice weekly to set and reflect on weekly goals, work on assessments, and participate in physiotherapy sessions. The program includes guest presenters and excursions. Focus is on athlete development and well-being as well as academic support, allowing students to manage their other academic commitments. Every two years the program has a major excursion interstate to visit other sporting schools and professional sporting facilities with guest speakers. Local students in the program benefit from close relationships with AIS scholarship students and their professional approach to study and sport. Students have access to both Tertiary and Accredited Course options.
eligibility_criteria: Elite athlete status; Current participation in high-performance sport; Years 11 and 12 enrollment; Demonstrated commitment to both academic and athletic pursuits
special_conditions: Focus is on athlete development and well-being as well as academic support. College does not directly train students in their specific sport but relies on elite training gained from outside school. Access to university library and University of Canberra facilities available to all students.

school_name: University of Canberra Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra
program_name: Australian Institute of Sport (AIS) Scholarship Program
overview: UC Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra is the designated school for AIS scholarship athletes in Years 11 and 12. The college enables elite athletes to pursue high-performance training while completing secondary education through a formal partnership between the Australian Sports Commission and ACT Government.
description: The AIS Scholarship Program at UC Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra accommodates elite athletes through flexible academic arrangements designed to align with demanding athletic schedules. Established under a long-standing partnership with the Australian Sports Commission, the program has been in place since the college's inception in 1987. Personalized study programs are negotiated between athletes, teachers, and AIS staff to support academic progress alongside training. Specialist tutors and an AIS Liaison Officer offer tailored support, including customized study packages and progress monitoring. Students meet ACT Board of Senior Secondary Studies requirements, with options to extend enrollment to a three-year program if competitions or injuries delay progress. Priority enrollment is extended to national team members and AIS-selected athletes to minimize disruptions from travel or selection camps. All students have access to AIS facilities, including specialized training venues and expert coaching resources.
eligibility_criteria: AIS scholarship athlete status; Enrolled in AIS training program; Years 11 and 12 students; Must meet ACT Board of Senior Secondary Studies requirements
special_conditions: Partnership established between Australian Sports Commission and ACT Government. Academic programs negotiated individually. Students may extend enrollment to three years if necessary. Flexible study options provided to accommodate training and competition schedules.

school_name: University of Canberra Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra
overview: UC Senior Secondary College Lake Ginninderra offers the Capital Chemist Awards in partnership with Public Education to recognize outstanding student achievement. These prestigious awards recognise excellence in academic achievement, citizenship, and meaningful contribution to the community.
description: The Capital Chemist Awards are presented in partnership with Public Education to celebrate students who demonstrate exceptional achievement across academic, citizenship, and community contribution dimensions. Recipients are recognized for what it means to truly embody the college's values through their commitment to learning, positive connections with others, and the impact they make within and beyond the classroom. The awards recognize students who have made a significant impact, whether through academic dedication, leadership, or service to others. Recipients represent the very best of the college community.
eligibility_criteria: Excellence in academic achievement; Demonstrated citizenship; Meaningful contribution to community; Embodiment of college values; Commitment to learning and positive connections with others
special_conditions: Partnership with Public Education. Recognition-based award for students demonstrating exceptional achievement across academic, citizenship and community service dimensions.
